Denis Phillips I am not a Floridian, just moved last year but as I have been following, you have been saying not to get anxious, so why did governor Di Santis declared a state of emergency?
What else is going on?

Declaring a state of emergency in advance allows greater speed and flexibility in reacting in case something bad does happen. While this is only expected to be a TS when it hits, it looks like it will impact large areas of the state and there could be flooding, especially since the ground is saturated in some places from the recent rainfall.
have a state of emergency been declared every time there a Major hurricane
Yes, doesn't even have to be a major hurricane. Can just be a tropical storm such as this case.
thanks
yes don’t let they worry you. They are just doing it as protection in case DeSantis needs to acquire funding from the US Govt
thanks

that’s a very good question. He does that early for a couple of reason. It allows FEMA money and resources to be active before the storm hits so we can get the aid and recovery vehicles ready to go before hand. It’s just a way to cut through the government red tape before hand, so we can get the help where it needs to be as soon as the storms over.
